subject: Ecology
title: Butterflies and Moths
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Butterflies and Moths.

hook.text: Butterflies pollinate with style—landing on bright flowers and uncoiling a long proboscis.

buildup.text: They favor flat, open flowers with landing platforms where they can perch while feeding.

discovery.text: Moths do the night shift—pollinating pale, fragrant flowers that open after sunset.

twist.text: Hawk moths hover like hummingbirds and pollinate deep tubular flowers with tongues over 30 cm long.

climax.text: Darwin predicted a moth with a 30 cm tongue to match a deep orchid—and it was found decades later.

punchline.text: Darwin predicted the pollinator before anyone found it.

quiz.question: What type of flowers do moths typically pollinate?
options: Pale, fragrant flowers that open at night
Bright red flowers during the day
